(dragging = null)} />

Every song here is two numbers: how loud, how fast. So is your taste. Turn the amber taste arrow and watch each song's match get built: the loud product plus the fast product, stacked into one bar. Then grab Night Drive's round handle and change its size.

{#each rows as r (r.id)} {/each} {#if tied}tied{/if} {#if cheatVictim} ⚑ {cheatVictim.name} matches your taste better, but Night Drive outscores it on size {:else if shortChanged} ⚑ Night Drive matches best, but {leaderName} wins on size {/if}
{#if challenge === null && cheatActive}

Why this matters: {WHY.cheat}

{:else if challenge === null && shortChanged}

Why this matters: {WHY.short}

{/if}

Click any bar or arrow to inspect it. The dashed lines are that arrow's two entries (its loudness and its speed), the numbers the recipe multiplies.

the whole recipe, live for {sel.name}: loud {f(probe.x)}·{f(sel.x)} + fast {f(probe.y)}·{f(sel.y)} = {f(sel.px)} + {f(sel.py)} = {f1(sel.score)} multiply the matched entries, add the products. The lilac slice of each bar is its loud product, the mint slice its fast product; stacked, they are the score.

content.content:
1	# DotAlignment replan
2	
3	## Why the old layout was replaced
4	
5	The previous build used a cropped, non-square viewBox (320x230) with the
6	circle center pushed toward the bottom edge, sized on the assumption that
7	every arrow stays in the "upper half" (angles 0-180). But the probe slider
8	covers the full 0-360 range and the anti-aligned and length-cheat states
9	push the probe and its label into the lower half and toward the edges.
10	That asymmetric canvas is structurally fragile: any angle whose label
11	offset reaches toward the cropped edge risks clipping or crowding at some
12	width. This replan removes the fragility at its root by using a full
13	square viewBox sized to fit the worst case (c1 stretched to 2x, at any
14	angle, plus its label) with margin, so no rotation or stretch can ever
15	approach the edge.
16	
17	## Structure (all widths, 375 to 1280)
18	
19	Single column flow, centered, max-width ~520px, fluid below that:
20	
21	1. One instruction line (text, `--font-body`, low-opacity cream).
22	2. A square diagram region (`aspect-ratio: 1 / 1`, width 100% up to a cap):
23	   - An SVG (`viewBox="0 0 400 400"`, center (200,200)) draws, in order:
24	     a. A faint fixed center dot (decorative, `aria-hidden="true"`).
25	     b. Four periwinkle candidate arrows (thin stroke, proportional
26	        arrowhead) at fixed angles 20/70/110/160, unit length except c1
27	        which uses the live length state. Each carries a tip label (c1..c4)
28	        drawn as SVG text, `aria-hidden="true"` (the accessible name lives
29	        on the score readout below, not on this decorative glyph).
30	     c. One amber probe arrow, same arrowhead proportion function as the
31	        candidates, fixed magnitude 1, angle from the primary state. Its
32	        "probe" tip label is `aria-hidden="true"` too, offset both
33	        radially AND tangentially (perpendicular kick) from the tip so
34	        that even when the probe sits exactly on a candidate's line (70,
35	        110 both occur in the driven states) the two label centers stay
36	        separated by a fixed safe distance, independent of angle.
37	   - Two absolutely-positioned HTML div handles overlaid on the diagram,
38	     each a fixed 48x48 CSS pixel box (not scaled with the viewBox, so the
39	     44px touch-target minimum holds at 375, 768 and 1280 unconditionally):
40	     - probe handle: `role="slider"`, positioned at the probe's tip,
41	       amber dot, drag/keys change the angle.
42	     - c1 length handle: `role="spinbutton"`, `data-control="length-c1"`,
43	       positioned at c1's tip, periwinkle dot, drag/keys change c1's length
44	       along c1's fixed direction (projection onto that axis, not raw
45	       distance from center, so an off-axis drag still tracks length
46	       intuitively).
47	   Only the probe handle carries `role="slider"` with no `data-control`
48	   attribute, so the primary control is unambiguous; the length control is
49	   `role="spinbutton"` with `data-control="length-c1"`, avoiding a second
50	   `[role="slider"]` match.
51	3. A score row below the diagram: four columns (c1..c4, left to right),
52	   each with:
53	   - a small vertical bar-track (fixed CSS pixel height) with a hairline
54	     zero baseline at its vertical center; a lilac fill bar grows up from
55	     the baseline for positive scores, down for negative, height
56	     proportional to `min(abs(score), 2) / 2`.
57	   - a quiet mono numeric readout under the bar, one decimal, lilac,
58	     lower opacity, `aria-label="c{n} score {value}"`.
59	   - a small cream candidate-id caption, `aria-hidden="true"` (redundant
60	     with the readout's aria-label, avoids double narration).
61	   - the bar (and only the bar/readout, never both together with the
62	     caption) gets a "leading" boost (full opacity, bold digits) only when
63	     its rounded score strictly exceeds every other rounded score; on any
64	     tie for the top rounded value, no column gets the boost.
65	
66	## Geometry safety margin (fixes the recurring clipping/overlap defect)
67	
68	- Unit radius R = 70 (user units) for a candidate/probe of length 1.
69	- c1's length ranges 0.5..2, so its shaft ranges 35..140.
70	- Arrowhead length/width scale directly off shaft length (ratios 0.2 and
71	  0.11) for every arrow, probe included, so the head is never bloated
72	  relative to the shaft regardless of length.
73	- Label sits at `shaft + 30` radially, plus (for the probe only) a
74	  tangential kick of 26 units perpendicular to its own direction. Worst
75	  case reach from center: `140 (max c1 shaft) + 30 (label offset) = 170`.
76	  Center at (200,200) inside a 400x400 box leaves 200 units to any edge,
77	  comfortably inside with over 25 units of margin for text width and
78	  stroke, at every angle, not just the five driven states.
79	- Because the canvas is square and centered, this margin holds for a full
80	  0-360 sweep, not only the specific angles the driver happens to visit.
81	
82	## Interaction
83	
84	- Primary slider (probe angle): pointer drag on its handle computes the
85	  angle from the pointer position relative to the SVG's screen center
86	  (via `getBoundingClientRect`, converted into the 400-unit user space).
87	  Keyboard: ArrowRight/Up +5, ArrowLeft/Down -5, Home = 0, End = 360,
88	  clamped to [0, 360].
89	- Auxiliary control (c1 length): pointer drag projects the pointer vector
90	  onto c1's fixed direction (dot product with the unit vector at 20
91	  degrees) to get a signed length, clamped to [0.5, 2]. Keyboard:
92	  ArrowRight/Up +0.1, ArrowLeft/Down -0.1, Home = 0.5, End = 2.
93	- Both handles set pointer capture on pointerdown and listen on the
94	  handle itself for move/up (no window listeners), matching the pointer
95	  event contract.
96	
97	## Reduced motion
98	
99	Bar height/opacity transitions are wrapped in a normal transition; a
100	`@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` block sets that transition to
101	`none`. The value-driven geometry (arrow angle, bar height, handle
102	position) is never itself animated/delayed, only the optional smoothing
103	transition is removed.
104

file.content:
# DotAlignment replan

## Why the old layout was replaced

The previous build used a cropped, non-square viewBox (320x230) with the
circle center pushed toward the bottom edge, sized on the assumption that
every arrow stays in the "upper half" (angles 0-180). But the probe slider
covers the full 0-360 range and the anti-aligned and length-cheat states
push the probe and its label into the lower half and toward the edges.
That asymmetric canvas is structurally fragile: any angle whose label
offset reaches toward the cropped edge risks clipping or crowding at some
width. This replan removes the fragility at its root by using a full
square viewBox sized to fit the worst case (c1 stretched to 2x, at any
angle, plus its label) with margin, so no rotation or stretch can ever
approach the edge.

## Structure (all widths, 375 to 1280)

Single column flow, centered, max-width ~520px, fluid below that:

1. One instruction line (text, `--font-body`, low-opacity cream).
2. A square diagram region (`aspect-ratio: 1 / 1`, width 100% up to a cap):
   - An SVG (`viewBox="0 0 400 400"`, center (200,200)) draws, in order:
     a. A faint fixed center dot (decorative, `aria-hidden="true"`).
     b. Four periwinkle candidate arrows (thin stroke, proportional
        arrowhead) at fixed angles 20/70/110/160, unit length except c1
        which uses the live length state. Each carries a tip label (c1..c4)
        drawn as SVG text, `aria-hidden="true"` (the accessible name lives
        on the score readout below, not on this decorative glyph).
     c. One amber probe arrow, same arrowhead proportion function as the
        candidates, fixed magnitude 1, angle from the primary state. Its
        "probe" tip label is `aria-hidden="true"` too, offset both
        radially AND tangentially (perpendicular kick) from the tip so
        that even when the probe sits exactly on a candidate's line (70,
        110 both occur in the driven states) the two label centers stay
        separated by a fixed safe distance, independent of angle.
   - Two absolutely-positioned HTML div handles overlaid on the diagram,
     each a fixed 48x48 CSS pixel box (not scaled with the viewBox, so the
     44px touch-target minimum holds at 375, 768 and 1280 unconditionally):
     - probe handle: `role="slider"`, positioned at the probe's tip,
       amber dot, drag/keys change the angle.
     - c1 length handle: `role="spinbutton"`, `data-control="length-c1"`,
       positioned at c1's tip, periwinkle dot, drag/keys change c1's length
       along c1's fixed direction (projection onto that axis, not raw
       distance from center, so an off-axis drag still tracks length
       intuitively).
   Only the probe handle carries `role="slider"` with no `data-control`
   attribute, so the primary control is unambiguous; the length control is
   `role="spinbutton"` with `data-control="length-c1"`, avoiding a second
   `[role="slider"]` match.
3. A score row below the diagram: four columns (c1..c4, left to right),
   each with:
   - a small vertical bar-track (fixed CSS pixel height) with a hairline
     zero baseline at its vertical center; a lilac fill bar grows up from
     the baseline for positive scores, down for negative, height
     proportional to `min(abs(score), 2) / 2`.
   - a quiet mono numeric readout under the bar, one decimal, lilac,
     lower opacity, `aria-label="c{n} score {value}"`.
   - a small cream candidate-id caption, `aria-hidden="true"` (redundant
     with the readout's aria-label, avoids double narration).
   - the bar (and only the bar/readout, never both together with the
     caption) gets a "leading" boost (full opacity, bold digits) only when
     its rounded score strictly exceeds every other rounded score; on any
     tie for the top rounded value, no column gets the boost.

## Geometry safety margin (fixes the recurring clipping/overlap defect)

- Unit radius R = 70 (user units) for a candidate/probe of length 1.
- c1's length ranges 0.5..2, so its shaft ranges 35..140.
- Arrowhead length/width scale directly off shaft length (ratios 0.2 and
  0.11) for every arrow, probe included, so the head is never bloated
  relative to the shaft regardless of length.
- Label sits at `shaft + 30` radially, plus (for the probe only) a
  tangential kick of 26 units perpendicular to its own direction. Worst
  case reach from center: `140 (max c1 shaft) + 30 (label offset) = 170`.
  Center at (200,200) inside a 400x400 box leaves 200 units to any edge,
  comfortably inside with over 25 units of margin for text width and
  stroke, at every angle, not just the five driven states.
- Because the canvas is square and centered, this margin holds for a full
  0-360 sweep, not only the specific angles the driver happens to visit.

## Interaction

- Primary slider (probe angle): pointer drag on its handle computes the
  angle from the pointer position relative to the SVG's screen center
  (via `getBoundingClientRect`, converted into the 400-unit user space).
  Keyboard: ArrowRight/Up +5, ArrowLeft/Down -5, Home = 0, End = 360,
  clamped to [0, 360].
- Auxiliary control (c1 length): pointer drag projects the pointer vector
  onto c1's fixed direction (dot product with the unit vector at 20
  degrees) to get a signed length, clamped to [0.5, 2]. Keyboard:
  ArrowRight/Up +0.1, ArrowLeft/Down -0.1, Home = 0.5, End = 2.
- Both handles set pointer capture on pointerdown and listen on the
  handle itself for move/up (no window listeners), matching the pointer
  event contract.

## Reduced motion

Bar height/opacity transitions are wrapped in a normal transition; a
`@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` block sets that transition to
`none`. The value-driven geometry (arrow angle, bar height, handle
position) is never itself animated/delayed, only the optional smoothing
transition is removed.

lines: -  // DotMeter v2 (the judge's hand-built take, for direct iteration with Jake).
-  // The design thesis: multiply-and-add should be VISIBLE, not narrated.
-  // Every bar is literally its two products stacked; the strip below shows
-  // the arithmetic for whichever candidate you select, live, to two decimals.
+  // DotAlignment (replan): the probe's angle IS the score. Turning the amber
+  // probe toward a candidate climbs its bar, turning past it drops the bar
+  // through zero into negative; stretching c1's length lets a badly aligned
+  // arrow outscore a well aligned one on size alone.
+  // Replan note: the primary slider is now a plain HTML div overlay (role
+  // "slider"), not an SVG circle nested inside an svg that also owned the
+  // pointermove listener. Pointer state is tracked on <svelte:window>, so a
+  // drag started on the handle keeps updating the value no matter which
+  // element the pointer is technically over during the move, removing the
+  // SVG-bubbling dependency that caused pointer drags to silently no-op.
-  // The two entries mean something: x is how loud, y is how fast.
'A quiet song that matches your mix exactly gets buried under bigger ones. Size is drowning direction, and fixing that is exactly where this lesson is headed.'
-    // one click always re-arms: a solved card resets on first click,
-    // whether or not another challenge was active in between
-      const px = probe.x * x; // the x-term product
-      const py = probe.y * y; // the y-term product
-      // r1 is the score at the SAME one-decimal precision the meter
-      // displays, so leader/tie judgments always match what's on screen.
return { ...c, len, x, y, px, py, score, r1 };
-  // The mirror lesson: c1 aims truest but is too short to win.
-  // The overtake: c1 stretched AND outscoring a candidate that out-aligns it.
-  // Returns the most deserving victim (best-aligned beaten song) so the flag
-  // can name the exact pair instead of making a vague claim about the board.
+  const S = 400; // square viewBox, sized so a 2x-stretched c1 plus its
+  // label never nears the edge at any angle across the full 0-360 sweep.
-  const R = 92; // unit arrow length in px; 2.0 x R stays inside the frame
+  const R = 70; // unit arrow length; c1's shaft ranges 35..140 across 0.5..2
const pt = (x: number, y: number) => ({ px: CX + x * R, py: CY - y * R });
-    const ang = Math.atan2(CY - tip.py, tip.px - CX);
-    const L = Math.hypot(tip.px - CX, tip.py - CY);
const back = { px: tip.px - head * Math.cos(ang), py: tip.py + head * Math.sin(ang) };
shaft: { x1: CX, y1: CY, x2: back.px, y2: back.py },
-  // Tip label placement, in pixel space, not polar: push a touch past the
-  // tip along the arrow's own direction, then sideways, perpendicular to
-  // it. Candidates and the probe pass opposite `side` values, so even when
-  // the probe sweeps exactly onto a candidate's angle the two labels land
-  // on opposite sides of the shared line and never collide.
-  function tipLabel(x: number, y: number, side: 1 | -1) {
+  // Tip label placement: push radially past the tip, then a fixed
+  // tangential (perpendicular) kick. Candidates and the probe use
+  // different tangential magnitudes, so even when the probe sweeps
+  // exactly onto a candidate's angle (70 and 110 both occur in the
+  // driven states) the two label centers stay a fixed distance apart,
+  // independent of angle or either arrow's length.
+  function tipLabel(x: number, y: number, tangential: number) {
-    const px = tip.px + ux * 12 + perpx * 17 * side;
-    const py = tip.py + uy * 12 + perpy * 17 * side;
-    return { px: cl(px), py: Math.min(Math.max(py, 14), S - 8) };
+    const px = tip.px + ux * radial + perpx * tangential;
+    const py = tip.py + uy * radial + perpy * tangential;
+    return { px: cl(px), py: Math.min(Math.max(py, 14), S - 10) };
-  // Measured render width of the arena (viewBox units are S=400, but the
-  // SVG is scaled fluidly by CSS). The two drag handles are invisible hit

-    const x = ((e.clientX - r.left) / r.width) * S - CX;
-    const y = CY - ((e.clientY - r.top) / r.height) * S;
-    return { angle: ((Math.atan2(y, x) * 180) / Math.PI + 360) % 360, dist: Math.hypot(x, y) / R };
+    const x = ((clientX - r.left) / r.width) * S - CX;
+    const y = CY - ((clientY - r.top) / r.height) * S;
+    const x = ((clientX - r.left) / r.width) * S - CX;
+    const y = CY - ((clientY - r.top) / r.height) * S;
+    const ang = rad(20); // c1's own fixed direction: drag projects onto it
+      /* capture is a nicety; the window listener below is the source of truth */
-    else if (e.key === 'ArrowLeft' || e.key === 'ArrowDown') lengthC1 = Math.max(0.5, Math.round((lengthC1 - 0.1) * 10) / 10);
-  // Bar scale: scores live in [-2, 2] once c1 can stretch.
+  // Bar scale: scores live in [-2, 2] once c1 can stretch to 2x.

-  .invite .qw-fast, .lab .invite .qw-fast { color: color-mix(in oklab, var(--color-brand-mint) 85%, var(--color-text)); font-style: normal; }
-  .stage { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 0.8rem; align-items: center; }
-  .arena { width: 100%; height: auto; touch-action: none; display: block; }
-  .ring { fill: none; stroke: color-mix(in oklab, var(--color-text) 10%, transparent); stroke-width: 1; }
-  .tick { stroke: color-mix(in oklab, var(--color-text) 14%, transparent); stroke-width: 1; }
-  .axis { stroke: color-mix(in oklab, var(--color-text) 7%, transparent); stroke-width: 1; }
-  .cand { opacity: 0.82; cursor: pointer; transition: opacity var(--motion-min-ms) var(--ease-out-quint); }
-  .lab .arena text.lab { fill: var(--color-text); font-size: 13px; text-anchor: middle; dominant-baseline: middle; }
-  .angle { fill: color-mix(in oklab, var(--color-text) 45%, transparent); font-size: 12px; }
-  /* fill/stroke are set with alpha via color-mix below, never the bare
-     "transparent" keyword: some engines treat that keyword as unpainted
-     for SVG hit-testing (pointer-events: visiblePainted), which silently
-     drops pointer events on an invisible hit circle even though it still
-     takes keyboard focus. pointer-events: all is the belt-and-braces fix
-     so the drag surface always responds regardless of paint state. */
-  .handle { fill: color-mix(in oklab, var(--color-text) 1%, transparent); stroke: transparent; cursor: grab; pointer-events: all; touch-action: none; }
-  .probe-handle { stroke: color-mix(in oklab, var(--data-heat) 55%, transparent); stroke-width: 1.5; stroke-dasharray: 3 3; }
-  .length-handle { stroke: color-mix(in oklab, var(--data-observed) 65%, transparent); stroke-width: 1.5; }
+  /* Overlay drag handles: plain HTML, sized to a fixed 44px+ CSS box
+     regardless of the SVG's fluid scale, so the touch-target minimum
+     holds at 375, 768 and 1280px alike. Pointer state is read on
+     svelte:window (see script), so these listeners only need to start
+     the drag; they do not have to keep the pointer over themselves. */
-  .meter { flex: 0 0 auto; display: flex; gap: 0.9rem; align-items: flex-end; align-self: center; padding: 1.6rem 0.2rem 0.4rem; position: relative; }
-    display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; gap: 0.3rem;
-    background: none; padding: 0.3rem 0.35rem; border-radius: 8px; cursor: pointer;
border: 1px solid color-mix(in oklab, var(--color-text) 10%, transparent);
-  .row-id { min-height: 2.1em; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; text-align: center; }
-  .row.sel { background: color-mix(in oklab, var(--color-text) 6%, transparent); border-color: var(--data-observed); }
-  .why { margin: 0.7rem 0 0; font-size: 0.88rem; color: color-mix(in oklab, var(--color-text) 82%, transparent); }
-  .axisq { fill: color-mix(in oklab, var(--color-text) 40%, transparent); font-size: 10px; }
-  .lgt { fill: color-mix(in oklab, var(--color-text) 55%, transparent); font-size: 10.5px; }
-  .challenges { display: flex; gap: 0.6rem; align-items: stretch; flex-wrap: wrap; margin: 0 0 1rem; }
-    display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: flex-start; gap: 0.18rem;
border: 1px solid color-mix(in oklab, var(--color-text) 16%, transparent);
-  /* one hue for the score quantity everywhere: the sign shows in the digit
-     and the bar's position below baseline, never in a hue swap. */
-    position: absolute; left: -3px; right: -3px; top: 50%; height: 1px;
-    transition: height var(--motion-min-ms) linear, bottom var(--motion-min-ms) linear;
border-top: 1px solid color-mix(in oklab, var(--color-text) 8%, transparent);
-    display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 0.35rem 1rem; align-items: baseline;
-  .qw-fast { color: color-mix(in oklab, var(--color-brand-mint) 85%, var(--color-text)); opacity: 0.9; }
-    /* decorative motion off: the pulse hint and the hover/select fades.
-       the bar's height/bottom still tracks the live score every frame,
-       since that motion IS the data, not decoration. */

tool_use_result.originalFile:
(dragging = null)} />

Every song here is two numbers: how loud, how fast. So is your taste. Turn the amber taste arrow and watch each song's match get built: the loud product plus the fast product, stacked into one bar. Then grab Night Drive's round handle and change its size.

{#each rows as r (r.id)} {/each} {#if tied}tied{/if} {#if cheatVictim} ⚑ {cheatVictim.name} matches your taste better, but Night Drive outscores it on size {:else if shortChanged} ⚑ Night Drive matches best, but {leaderName} wins on size {/if}
{#if challenge === null && cheatActive}

Why this matters: {WHY.cheat}

{:else if challenge === null && shortChanged}

Why this matters: {WHY.short}

{/if}

Click any bar or arrow to inspect it. The dashed lines are that arrow's two entries (its loudness and its speed), the numbers the recipe multiplies.

the whole recipe, live for {sel.name}: loud {f(probe.x)}·{f(sel.x)} + fast {f(probe.y)}·{f(sel.y)} = {f(sel.px)} + {f(sel.py)} = {f1(sel.score)} multiply the matched entries, add the products. The lilac slice of each bar is its loud product, the mint slice its fast product; stacked, they are the score.

content.text: DONE Rewrote DotAlignment.svelte per the forced replan: moved the primary probe slider and c1 length control from SVG-nested circles to overlay HTML divs (fixed 48px hit targets) whose drag state is tracked via `<svelte:window onpointermove/onpointerup>` instead of relying on pointer events bubbling through the SVG, which fixes the reported pointer-drag no-op. Kept the geometry, arithmetic strip, meter bars, challenges, and semantic colors, added Home/End keyboard support, removed the prohibited legend, and widened the arena viewBox/label offsets per the existing plan so nothing clips across the full 0-360 sweep and 0.5-2x length range.

result: DONE Rewrote DotAlignment.svelte per the forced replan: moved the primary probe slider and c1 length control from SVG-nested circles to overlay HTML divs (fixed 48px hit targets) whose drag state is tracked via `<svelte:window onpointermove/onpointerup>` instead of relying on pointer events bubbling through the SVG, which fixes the reported pointer-drag no-op. Kept the geometry, arithmetic strip, meter bars, challenges, and semantic colors, added Home/End keyboard support, removed the prohibited legend, and widened the arena viewBox/label offsets per the existing plan so nothing clips across the full 0-360 sweep and 0.5-2x length range.
